title: \alpha-Gauss Curvature flows with flat sides
summary:
In this paper, we study the deformation of the 2 dimensional convex surfaces
in $\R^{3}$ whose speed at a point on the surface is proportional to
$\alpha$-power of positive part of Gauss Curvature. First, for 1/2<\alpha\leq
1$, we show that there is smooth solution if the initial data is smooth and
strictly convex and that there is a viscosity solution with $C^{1,1}$-estimate
before the collapsing time if the initial surface is only convex. Moreover, we
show that there is a waiting time effect which means the flat spot of the
convex surface will persist for a while. We also show the interface between the
flat side and the strictly convex side of the surface remains smooth on $0 < t
< T_0$ under certain necessary regularity and non-degeneracy initial
conditions, where $ T_0$ is the vanishing time of the flat side.

title: The Limit of the Boltzmann Equation to the Euler Equations for Riemann   Problems
summary:
The convergence of the Boltzmann equaiton to the compressible Euler equations
when the Knudsen number tends to zero has been a long standing open problem in
the kinetic theory. In the setting of Riemann solution that contains the
generic superposition of shock, rarefaction wave and contact discontinuity to
the Euler equations, we succeed in justifying this limit by introducing
hyperbolic waves with different solution backgrounds to capture the extra
masses carried by the hyperbolic approximation of the rarefaction wave and the
diffusion approximation of contact discontinuity.

title: Comparability and regularity estimates for symmetric nonlocal Dirichlet   forms
summary:
The aim of this work is to study comparability of nonlocal Dirichlet forms.
We provide sufficient conditions on the kernel for local and global
comparability. As an application we prove a-priori estimates in H\"{o}lder
spaces for solutions to integrodifferential equations. These solutions are
defined with the help of symmetric nonlocal Dirichlet forms.
